title: vite.config.ts uses @ts-expect-error for codeSplitting — fragile on Vite upgrades
evidence: // @ts-expect-error Vite 8 types lack codeSplitting but it exists at runtime codeSplitting: false,
suggestion: Suppress with a type assertion instead: (build as any).codeSplitting = false; or define the option in a separate config object with index signature. At minimum document which Vite version this was tested with.

notes.text: Cast build config object to any instead of @ts-expect-error. TypeScript compiles cleanly, no suppressed errors.
